crop: Striped yellow beetles on corn / cucurbits

graph.confusable_reason: Adult western corn rootworm and striped cucumber beetle are both elongate yellow beetles with longitudinal dark stripes on the elytra.
graph.shared_presentation: An elongated yellow beetle with longitudinal dark stripes on the wing covers (elytra).

forks.question: Examine the stripe pattern on the elytra.
forks.a_signal: Stripes broader, mottled or blurry, sometimes merging so the back looks darker, and often not extending all the way to the elytra tip.
forks.b_signal: Three clean, narrow, high-contrast stripes extending nearly to the tip of the elytra.

forks.question: Zoom on the legs: are they black or yellow?
forks.a_signal: Legs entirely or almost entirely black.
forks.b_signal: Legs mostly yellow with only the joints darkened.

forks.question: If visible, check the underside of the abdomen.
Judge overall body size and color.
forks.a_signal: Slightly larger, often with darker overall elytra in males.
forks.b_signal: Slightly smaller, bright lemon-yellow with very crisp stripes.

graph.narrative_a: Because the beetle has mottled stripes that do not reach the elytra tip, all-black legs, and a yellow underside, I classify it as western corn rootworm rather than striped cucumber beetle.
graph.narrative_b: Because the beetle has three crisp narrow stripes reaching the elytra tip, yellow legs with darkened joints only, and a black underside, I classify it as a striped cucumber beetle rather than western corn rootworm.
graph.management_a: Western corn rootworm: manage larvae in corn with rotation and Bt/soil insecticide; adults on silks seldom need treatment.
graph.management_b: Striped cucumber beetle: protect seedling cucurbits with row covers or labeled insecticide because it vectors bacterial wilt.
